content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN intervals where the ithi^{th}ith interval is [Li,Ri][L_i,R_i][Li‚Äã,Ri‚Äã].

In one operation, you will:


Choose an integer MMM (1‚â§M‚â§N)(1 \leq M \leq N)(1‚â§M‚â§N) and select MMM **distinct** indices x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xMx_1,x_2,\ldots,x_Mx1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,xM‚Äã.
For the ithi^{th}ith selected index xix_ixi‚Äã (corresponding to range [Lxi,Rxi][L_{x_i},R_{x_i}][Lxi‚Äã‚Äã,Rxi‚Äã‚Äã]):

Choose an integer KiK_iKi‚Äã where (1<Ki‚â§(Rxi‚àíLxi+1))(1 \lt K_i \leq (R_{x_i}-L_{x_i}+1))(1<Ki‚Äã‚â§(Rxi‚Äã‚Äã‚àíLxi‚Äã‚Äã+1));
Pick KiK_iKi‚Äã **distinct** integers Y(i,1),Y(i,2),‚Ä¶,Y(i,Ki)Y_{(i,1)},Y_{(i,2)},\ldots,Y_{(i,{K_i})}Y(i,1)‚Äã,Y(i,2)‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Y(i,Ki‚Äã)‚Äã from the range [Lxi,Rxi][L_{x_i},R_{x_i}][Lxi‚Äã‚Äã,Rxi‚Äã‚Äã].


We define the following for an operation:


The cost is defined as the product of all KiK_iKi‚Äã. Formally, cost =‚àèi=1MKi= \displaystyle\prod_{i=1}^M K_i=i=1‚àèM‚ÄãKi‚Äã
The score is defined as the product of all picked integers over all chosen intervals.

Formally, score =‚àèi=1M‚àèj=1KiY(i,j)= \displaystyle\prod_{i=1}^M \displaystyle\prod_{j=1}^{K_i}Y_{(i,j)}=i=1‚àèM‚Äãj=1‚àèKi‚Äã‚ÄãY(i,j)‚Äã.
An operation is good if:


Maximum number of intervals are chosen.

In other words, the value of MMM is the maximum possible.
The cost of the operation is **not greater** than NNN;
Your task is to find the **sum** of scores of all possible good operations ‚Äämod‚Ää‚ÄÖ‚Ää998244353\bmod\;998244353mod998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the number of intervals.
Following NNN lines contain two space-separated integers each, Li,RiL_i,R_iLi‚Äã,Ri‚Äã, describing the interval.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **sum** of the scores of all possible good operations ‚Äämod‚Ää‚ÄÖ‚Ää998244353\bmod\; 998244353mod998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: You can only choose M=1M = 1M=1 indices. Otherwise, the cost will be greater than N=3N=3N=3.
All the good operations are:

x1=1:x_1 = 1 :x1‚Äã=1: For the chosen interval [2,3][2,3][2,3], pick K=2K = 2K=2 distinct integers {2,3}\{2,3\}{2,3} in it.
The cost is 222 and the score is 2‚ãÖ3=62\cdot 3=62‚ãÖ3=6.
x1=2:x_1 = 2 :x1‚Äã=2: For the chosen interval [1,2][1,2][1,2], pick K=2K = 2K=2 distinct integers {1,2}\{1,2\}{1,2} in it.
The cost is 222 and the score is 1‚ãÖ2=21\cdot 2=21‚ãÖ2=2.
x1=3:x_1 = 3 :x1‚Äã=3: For the chosen interval [2,4][2,4][2,4], pick K=2K = 2K=2 distinct integers {2,3}\{2,3\}{2,3} in it.
The cost is 222 and the score is 2‚ãÖ3=62\cdot 3=62‚ãÖ3=6.
x1=3:x_1 = 3 :x1‚Äã=3: For the chosen interval [2,4][2,4][2,4], pick K=2K = 2K=2 distinct integers {2,4}\{2,4\}{2,4} in it.
The cost is 222 and the score is 2‚ãÖ4=82\cdot 4=82‚ãÖ4=8.
x1=3:x_1 = 3 :x1‚Äã=3: For the chosen interval [2,4][2,4][2,4], pick K=2K = 2K=2 distinct integers {3,4}\{3,4\}{3,4} in it.
The cost is 222 and the score is 3‚ãÖ4=123\cdot 4=123‚ãÖ4=12.
x1=3:x_1 = 3 :x1‚Äã=3: For the chosen interval [2,4][2,4][2,4], pick K=3K = 3K=3 distinct integers {2,3,4}\{2,3,4\}{2,3,4} in it.
The cost is 333 and the score is 2‚ãÖ3‚ãÖ4=242\cdot 3\cdot 4=242‚ãÖ3‚ãÖ4=24.

The sum of the scores of all possible good operations is 6+2+6+8+12+24=586+2+6+8+12+24=586+2+6+8+12+24=58.
